data_json.content:
what is the problem with the entity search in ticket-mcp? You are having issues with the workspace argument and finding tickets in a nested workspace.

ticket-mcp should not require a workspace parameter for the get call at all, it should search in all detected workspaces when it is omitted. if the workspace is given, it should also search the child workspaces for the id. The error return message, if no ticket is found, should include the workspaces that were searched.

the workspace parameter should usually not be passed, the command will search in the current directory. the workspace directory is only required for creation commands, as these should explicitly name where they should be created to prevent silent creations at the wrong root.

arguments.description:
# Ticket-domain transport workspace-resolution parity (focused first run)

The first parity run. Establishes the shared-resolver adoption + pure-transport audit pattern that the spec/rule/audit domains will reuse.

## Problem

`ticket-mcp` requires a `workspace` argument for `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description`. It resolves only that store before prefix lookup, so an explicitly supplied parent/root selector can fail to locate tickets indexed from descendant stores. The resulting not-found message does not identify the stores searched.

## Scope

- Make the `workspace` field optional for ticket-MCP read-by-ID inputs.
- When omitted, resolve reads against the server's current aggregate workspace root.
- When supplied, open the selected root and search its indexed descendant scan roots.
- Include the root plus descendant workspaces searched in no-match errors.
- Preserve explicit workspace requirements for creation commands, preventing accidental ticket creation in an inferred root.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description` accept `{ "id": "..." }` without a workspace parameter and resolve tickets present in the configured aggregate root.
2. Supplying a parent workspace/root still resolves tickets in an indexed descendant store.
3. A missing ID/prefix error reports all workspace roots searched.
4. Creation remains explicitly scoped by required `workspace` input.
5. Unit tests cover omitted workspace, descendant lookup, and searched-root diagnostics.

## Depends on

- `ef0ebf38-7f55-4bd7-bf0e-0b416650ee0b` — shared memory-api descendant-discovery helper (CLI groundwork) is reused here for mcp/http.

arguments.title: ticket-mcp read workspace resolution
arguments.body:
# Summary

Ticket-MCP ticket-ID read operations resolve against the configured aggregate ticket root by default, including indexed descendant stores, while creation remains explicitly rooted.

## Requirements

- `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description` accept an omitted workspace selector.
- An omitted selector resolves to the server index root.
- A supplied selector resolves that store and its indexed descendants.
- No-match diagnostics report every root searched.
- Create inputs retain required workspace selectors.

## Validation

- Add unit coverage for omitted-workspace reads, descendant ticket resolution, and no-match diagnostics.
- Run `cargo test -p ticket-mcp`.

## Related Implementation Ticket

- `27558fde-37b0-43eb-86c6-cfbe2d99a0b8`.

arguments.description:
# Ticket-domain transport workspace-resolution parity (focused first run)

The first parity run. Establishes the shared-resolver adoption + pure-transport audit pattern that the spec/rule/audit domains will reuse.

## Problem

`ticket-mcp` required a `workspace` argument for `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description`. It resolved only that store before prefix lookup, so a caller had to redundantly choose a workspace for a read and could not tell which aggregate roots had been searched when a prefix failed.

## Implemented behavior

- Read-by-ID MCP inputs now allow an omitted workspace and default to the server aggregate root.
- An explicitly supplied workspace is still opened as the requested root, retaining its indexed descendant scan roots for the lookup.
- Prefix misses report the scan-root paths searched.
- Creation remains explicitly scoped by its required workspace input.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description` accept `{ "id": "..." }` without a workspace parameter and resolve tickets present in the configured aggregate root.
2. Supplying a parent workspace/root still resolves tickets in an indexed descendant store.
3. A missing ID/prefix error reports all workspace roots searched.
4. Creation remains explicitly scoped by required `workspace` input.
5. Unit tests cover omitted workspace, descendant lookup, and searched-root diagnostics.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p ticket-mcp` — passed, 18 tests.
- VS Code diagnostics — no errors in the three modified ticket-MCP files.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

## Related spec

- `3fd3aaff-b0d9-494b-8bbb-802d71140d59` — ticket-mcp read workspace resolution.

## Depends on

- `ef0ebf38-7f55-4bd7-bf0e-0b416650ee0b` — shared memory-api descendant-discovery helper (CLI groundwork) is reused here for mcp/http.

arguments.body:
# Summary

Ticket-MCP ticket-ID read operations resolve against the configured aggregate ticket root by default, including indexed descendant stores, while creation remains explicitly rooted.

## Requirements

- `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description` accept an omitted workspace selector.
- An omitted selector resolves to the server index root.
- A supplied selector resolves that store and its indexed descendants.
- No-match diagnostics report every root searched.
- Create inputs retain required workspace selectors.

## Implementation

`TicketRefInput.workspace` is optional only for read-by-ID operations. `TicketServer::resolve_uuid_for_read` resolves prefixes through the selected aggregate `TicketStore` and appends persisted scan-root paths to no-match diagnostics.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p ticket-mcp` passed: 18 tests.
- VS Code diagnostics reported no errors in the modified Rust sources.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Related Implementation Ticket

- `27558fde-37b0-43eb-86c6-cfbe2d99a0b8`.

# Ticket-domain transport workspace-resolution parity (focused first run)

The first parity run. Establishes the shared-resolver adoption + pure-transport audit pattern that the spec/rule/audit domains will reuse.

## Problem

`ticket-mcp` required a `workspace` argument for `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description`. It resolved only that store before prefix lookup, so a caller had to redundantly choose a workspace for a read and could not tell which aggregate roots had been searched when a prefix failed.

## Implemented behavior

- Read-by-ID MCP inputs now allow an omitted workspace and default to the server aggregate root.
- An explicitly supplied workspace is still opened as the requested root, retaining its indexed descendant scan roots for the lookup.
- Prefix misses report the scan-root paths searched.
- Creation remains explicitly scoped by its required workspace input.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description` accept `{ "id": "..." }` without a workspace parameter and resolve tickets present in the configured aggregate root.
2. Supplying a parent workspace/root still resolves tickets in an indexed descendant store.
3. A missing ID/prefix error reports all workspace roots searched.
4. Creation remains explicitly scoped by required `workspace` input.
5. Unit tests cover omitted workspace, descendant lookup, and searched-root diagnostics.

## Validation

- `cargo test -p ticket-mcp` — passed, 18 tests.
- VS Code diagnostics — no errors in the three modified ticket-MCP files.
- `git diff --check` — passed.

## Review blocker

The ticket remains `in-implementation` until dependency `ef0ebf38-7f55-4bd7-bf0c-0b416650ee0b` progresses from `in-implementation`; the ticket lifecycle correctly prevents transition to `in-review` before then.

## Related spec

- `3fd3aaff-b0d9-494b-8bbb-802d71140d59` — ticket-mcp read workspace resolution.

## Depends on

- `ef0ebf38-7f55-4bd7-bf0c-0b416650ee0b` — shared memory-api descendant-discovery helper (CLI groundwork) is reused here for mcp/http.

data_json.content:
The problem was exactly in `ticket-mcp`’s read path: `TicketRefInput.workspace` was mandatory, and `get_ticket` / `get_ticket_description` opened only that selected store before resolving the ID prefix. That made callers pass redundant workspace selectors and gave a bare prefix-not-found error with no indication of the aggregate roots inspected.

Updated [memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs), [memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs), and [memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/query.rs](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/query.rs):

- `workspace` is optional for `get_ticket` and `get_ticket_description`; omission defaults to the server’s aggregate root.
- Explicit workspace selectors still open the requested store and search its indexed descendant roots.
- Prefix misses now include `searched workspaces: ...` with the persisted scan-root paths.
- Creation inputs remain explicitly workspace-scoped.

Added regression coverage for omitted-workspace lookup, descendant ticket lookup, and missing-prefix diagnostics. `cargo test -p ticket-mcp` passes with 18 tests; VS Code diagnostics and `git diff --check` are clean.

The work is tracked in [27558fde-37b0-43eb-86c6-cfbe2d99a0b8](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/27558fde-37b0-43eb-86c6-cfbe2d99a0b8) and specified in [3fd3aaff-b0d9-494b-8bbb-802d71140d59](memory-api/.spec/specs/3fd3aaff-b0d9-494b-8bbb-802d71140d59). The ticket remains `in-implementation` because its prerequisite `ef0ebf38-7f55-4bd7-bf0c-0b416650ee0b` has not advanced to review.
